title: Report-surface readability, clipping, and visible output QA
originSignal: A report workbook can be structurally valid while visible sheets are hard to read, clipped, empty, or not presentation-ready.
riskPattern: Static formulas and links can pass while titles, labels, charts, and important numbers are visually broken in the final workbook.
regressionChecks: Generate a sanitized report workbook with one clean report sheet, one clipped-text report sheet, and one blank report sheet.
Identify visible report sheets and key visible ranges before publish cleanup.
Detect blank or nearly blank report sheets.
Detect long text in narrow visible cells as a clipping/readability risk.
Confirm at least one clean report sheet has a chart and print area.
expectedEvidence: Fixture metadata records expected report sheets and expected finding codes.
Visual QA report returns readiness blocked-for-delivery for the mixed clean/risky fixture.
Visual QA report records 3 report sheets, 1 blank report sheet, 1 report sheet with chart evidence, and at least the long-text-narrow-column, blank-report-sheet, and missing-print-area finding codes.
Workbook surface inventory can still be used as supporting structural evidence.
boundaries: This fixture is workbook-backed but still uses static OpenXML visual QA checks.
It does not render pixels, export PDF, or prove exact Excel desktop display at every zoom level.
Windows Excel COM or manual screenshot/PDF review is still required when the final claim depends on rendered appearance.
nextLiveWorkbookRequirement: Add a Windows Excel COM or screenshot/PDF-backed visual render check for the same sanitized fixture.
